title: State-file archive architecture — separate live state from historical record across the whole pipeline
do_not_implement: DO NOT modify any state file, prompt, or script. Operator reviews and authorizes implementation in a separate step. Treat as a pure design exercise — the migration is staged across multiple subsequent operator-authorized commits, one per file.
summary: PROP-021's instrumentation surfaced one bloat case (priority-queue.json: 50KB on disk, 981B live state). The same shape lives in at least nine other agent state files across the pipeline. The pattern: one canonical file per concept carries BOTH 'live state I need to act on' AND 'every historical item that was ever live state' — and fast-path readers (dispatcher checks, OBE lookups, severity gates) pay the historical-record cost on every read. This PROP designs ONE unified archive convention applied across all bloat-affected files: live state stays in <name>.json (small, fast), historical record moves to <name>-archive.jsonl (append-only JSON-Lines, FUSE-friendly). Migration is per-file, atomic-per-file, ordered low-risk → high-risk. PROP-009r2 audit, decider lifecycle verification, integrity coverage checks, and operator workflow are each preserved by explicit design — see consumer_protection_matrix. The convention is documented as a new reference doc (monitor/prompts/reference/state-file-archives.md) so future state files inherit it correctly. PROP-018 sync-manifest absorbs the new archive files via consistent classifications. This is the broadened, pipeline-wide application of PROP-021's Option C (deferred); it makes the bloat fix structural, not one-off.

in_scope: monitor/decisions/human-notes.json, monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json, monitor/analyst/human-notes.json, monitor/social/human-notes.json (4 files, 525KB total, ~95KB live).
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json (14KB, all history; plus four redundant arrays — schema-cleanup byproduct).
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json (66KB; points array carries 197 review-history records).
monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl (529KB; already JSONL-shaped — verify only, no migration needed).
out_of_scope: monitor/decisions/open-issues.json — already a live-only file by design (5 closed-status leaks observed are decider hygiene bugs, not architecture).
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json — per-file-per-review, already correctly sized. PROP-019/020 fingerprint compare reads these (off-limits per directive non_goals).
monitor/decisions/applied-patches/*.json — per-file-per-patch directory, already the pattern to emulate.
monitor/analyst/new-wins/*.json, expansions/*.json, fingerprints/*.json, etc. — append-only directories, already correctly shaped.
data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json — canonical content data, no live/history split applies.
Schema changes to the live-state portion of any file beyond what's needed for the archive split.
Other architectural cleanups (open-issues hygiene, processed-reviews schema dedup outside the archive scope, etc.) — focused narrowly on the bloat pattern.

audit_pass.method: Walked candidate state files. For each: total bytes, decomposed into live-portion vs history-portion vs schema-overhead, identified primary readers and writers, characterized read-pattern (full-iteration vs lookup-by-ID vs status-filter), measured 'bloat impact' = approx live-state-readers-per-day × wasted-bytes-per-read.

monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.live_definition: queue array (currently 0 items). Fast-path readers want yes/no on whether queue has un-reviewed items.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.history_definition: history array (currently 175 entries, capped at 200 by PROP-009r2; popped queue items with pop_reason, claimed_review_file, popped_at, operator_bypass_reason).
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.schema_overhead: description, mode, mode_legal_values, mode_rules, writers, readers, schedule_state, last_updated.
writers: decider (Step E2 pop & E5 mode flip)
readers: PROP-009r2 3-day rolling audit reader (in decider Step E2 enforcement check + tinker Mode 2 shadow log audit)
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.read_pattern: queue: full iteration. history: only the audit reader iterates; everyone else ignores history.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Curmudgeon ~16 runs/day × 50KB read = 800KB/day. ~95% of that is history bytes the dispatcher does not consult.

monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.history_definition: notes[] entries with status='consumed' (31) or status='superseded' (1).
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Decider ~6 runs/day × 113KB read = 678KB/day. ~97% is consumed notes.

monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Curmudgeon ~16 runs/day × 39KB read = 624KB/day. 100% consumed bytes today.

monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.live_definition: notes[] with status='pending' or no status (currently 1 with no status field — likely operator append-in-progress).
monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Analyst ~12 runs/day × 338KB read = 4.0MB/day. Largest single bloat source by daily byte volume.

monitor/social/human-notes.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Social ~1 run/day × 36KB read = 36KB/day. Smallest of the four human-notes.

monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.live_definition: (none — every entry is, by definition, closed; the file is pure archive).
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.history_definition: issues[] with status in {fixed, wontfix, closed-wontfix, closed-fixed, closed, superseded, stale, stale-already-fixed, closed-duplicate, fixed-pending-verification} (1514 entries).
readers: analyst (every run, OBE lookup by ISS-NNN regex)
decider (intake dedup, lifecycle verification by id)
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.read_pattern: lookup-by-id (find single record). NO consumer iterates the array. Bulk-OBE pattern is one regex grep, then per-id targeted reads.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Cumulative across analyst (~12 runs/day), curmudgeon (~16/day), decider (~6/day), social (~1/day) = 35 runs/day × 1.4MB = 49MB/day of read tokens. Highest daily token cost.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.fits_directive_rule: Operator's stated rule: 'If the file is read on every dispatcher cycle, anything not actively-consulted is archive.' Every entry is closed; no entry is live. Therefore the entire file is archive.

monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.live_definition: (none — every entry is a 'review filename already processed' marker).
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.history_definition: Four overlapping arrays (legacy schema duplication): processed (457 filenames), section_reviews_processed (2), holistic_reviews_processed (5), processed_reviews (71), processed_files (1). The 'processed' array is canonical per DATA-SCHEMAS.md.
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.read_pattern: membership check: 'has filename X been processed?'
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Decider ~6 runs/day × 14KB read = 84KB/day. Modest but file is also a schema-cleanup target (note: schema dedup outside scope per directive non_goals; only the live/archive split is in scope here).

monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.live_definition: holistic_checks array (9 entries, ~1.3KB; rotation pre-Priority 4) + points[] entries with status='pending' (16 of 197, ~5KB) + cycle/last_run/last_review_time/etc. headers (~700B).
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.history_definition: points[] entries with status='reviewed' (153) or no status (28 — likely legacy entries pre-status-field). Total ~48KB.
readers: curmudgeon (every run, Step 4 holistic rotation; pre-PROP-020 also fingerprint compare)
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.read_pattern: holistic_checks: filter by status='pending'. points[]: post-PROP-020 the fingerprint compare moved to drift-audit; current readers only check 'is this win/section already in points with last_reviewed_cycle ≥ N' (lookup-by-id or filter-by-status).

monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.live_definition: items[] with status in {pending, draft_awaiting_human_group_review, operator-approved, DRAFT_AWAITING_OPERATOR_REVIEW} = 9 actionable + revised states (~30KB).
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.history_definition: items[] with status in {complete (266), integrated (4), cancelled (2), superseded (2), subsumed (3)} = 277 items (~350KB).
writers: analyst (append new EXP, status: pending; later updates)
decider (status: integrated, integrated_at; PROP-014-amendment additive edits)
readers: analyst (every run, dispatcher precheck — filter by status='pending'+!blocked_on; mode-2b re-examination of EXP)
decider (every run, integration loop — filter by status='complete'/'revised'+!integrated)
structure-integrity (daily, walk for collisions and category coverage)
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.read_pattern: filter by status. NO consumer iterates terminal-state items in the fast path; integrated/cancelled/superseded/subsumed are dead weight for ALL fast-path readers. structure-integrity walks the full array for collision detection but that's daily, not per-run.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.bloat_impact_per_day: Analyst+decider+integrity ~18 runs/day × 381KB = 6.86MB/day. Second-highest daily token cost after closed-issues.

monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.live_definition: items with status in {pending (33), open (2)} = 35 items (~21KB).
writers: decider (push items needing analyst re-examination, with related_issues, reason)
readers: analyst (every run, dispatcher precheck — filter by status='pending'/'open')
monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.read_pattern: filter by status. structure-shape is array-at-root (no top-level wrapper).

monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.j.live_definition: (N/A — this is an append-only log, no live/history distinction).
monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.j.read_pattern: tail-N or grep-by-path. No full-iteration in the fast path.
monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.j.verification: ALREADY archive-shaped per the convention proposed below. No migration needed. Recommend a future PROP add bounded retention (truncate after 90 days) — out of scope here.
monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.j.bloat_impact_per_day: Tinker daily × 529KB = 529KB/day. Tolerable; flagged as future-PROP target.

totals.estimated_daily_read_token_cost_today: ~64MB/day across all dispatchers (closed-issues 49MB + expansion-tracker 7MB + analyst-notes 4MB + tracker 1MB + others)
totals.estimated_daily_read_token_cost_post_mig: ~3MB/day (live-only files are tiny; archive only read on lookup-by-id grep, which streams not loads)

ranked_priority_for_migration.rationale: Already correct shape — verify-only step. Lowest risk, validates the convention.
Smallest of the human-notes; only one reader (social, daily). Validates the human-notes pattern with minimum blast radius.
Two readers (curmudgeon dispatcher + Step 0c2 cross-read by curmudgeon itself); 100% consumed today so migration is mostly schema.
One reader (decider). 1 pending note today (drain-mode); migration must preserve operator's pending note in the live file.
Largest human-notes (338KB). Two readers (analyst + curmudgeon Step 0c2). High savings, modest risk.
Canonical bloat case from PROP-021 instrumentation. PROP-009r2 audit reader is the load-bearing consumer that needs careful migration.
Single reader, status-filter pattern. Array-at-root shape requires slight migration variant.
Multi-writer, multiple status patterns inside points[]. Higher risk; migrate after all simpler files are validated.
381KB, 4 readers, write-verify discipline (Discipline 1a) depends on integrated_at field. Higher risk than tracker.json.
Migrate together with closed-issues (both have lookup-by-id semantics; convention proves out).

HIGHEST RISK. 1.4MB, 4 readers, every reader uses lookup-by-id. Last because if anything is going to surprise us, it's the bulk-OBE reader pattern in analyst, and we want maximum prior-validation of the convention.

unified_archive_convention.name: live-state / append-archive split
unified_archive_convention.headline: <name>.json holds ONLY items meeting the file's live-state predicate. <name>-archive.jsonl holds the full historical record (one JSON object per line, append-only).

naming_rule.live_file: <name>.json — unchanged path; same as today's file, but content scope shrinks to live-state-only.
naming_rule.archive_file: <name>-archive.jsonl — sibling file, same directory, same basename + '-archive' suffix + '.jsonl' extension.
naming_rule.rationale: JSONL is operationally simpler than a directory of per-event files: one open() per consumer; grep-friendly; jq-friendly; tail-friendly; FUSE no-unlink-friendly (append-only never unlinks). Per-event-files give finer git-blame granularity but at the cost of fs.readdir() + per-file open() and inode pressure. JSONL preserves git-blame-per-line, which is enough granularity for forensics.

content_shape.live_file_shape: Identical to today's schema, except item arrays only contain live-state items per the file's live-state predicate. Top-level metadata (description, writers, readers, mode, last_updated, etc.) unchanged. The shape is a strict subset of today's file.
content_shape.archive_file_shape: One JSON object per line, no enclosing array. Each line carries the FULL record at the moment it was archived (i.e., includes the terminal-state fields like status:'consumed', consumed_at, consumed_by, popped_at, claimed_review_file, etc.). Records are immutable post-write.
content_shape.no_indices_by_default: No separate index file. Lookup-by-id is `grep '"id":"X"' <archive>` then JSON.parse the matching line. Indices invite their own staleness bug; grep-on-1.4MB is sub-100ms wall-clock at the FS level.
content_shape.future_index_path: If profiling shows lookup-by-id is hot enough to warrant indexing, add <name>-archive-index.json with {id: byte_offset} mapping. Defer until measured need.

append_vs_mutate_semantics.live_file: Mutate freely. Writers add/remove/modify items in the live array; only items in the live-state predicate are kept. Same write semantics as today.
append_vs_mutate_semantics.archive_file: APPEND-ONLY via fs.appendFileSync. Never mutate, never rewrite, never delete. Each line is immutable once written.
append_vs_mutate_semantics.transaction_pattern: When an item transitions from live → terminal: (1) append the FULL terminal record to archive jsonl, (2) remove the item from live file, (3) commit both files in the same git commit. Order matters: append-first-remove-second so a crash between (1) and (2) leaves a duplicate in live (recoverable) rather than a vanished record in neither (unrecoverable).

reader_pattern.live_state_reader: JSON.parse(readFileSync(<name>.json)) — same code as today, file is just smaller.
reader_pattern.lookup_by_id_reader: execSync(`grep '"id":"${X}"' <name>-archive.jsonl | head -1`) → JSON.parse the line. Or: read line-by-line via readline interface, filter, return first match.
reader_pattern.iteration_reader: Streamed read line-by-line (Node's readline or `fs.createReadStream + split('\n')`); JSON.parse each line. Memory footprint stays O(1) regardless of archive size.

operator_workflow_contract.for_human_notes: Operator ALWAYS appends to <name>.json (the live file). The agent that consumes the note (decider, analyst, curmudgeon, social) does the live→archive move when it processes the note. Operator never writes directly to *-archive.jsonl. This is uniform across ALL human-notes files — no file-specific exception.
operator_workflow_contract.for_other_files: Operator does NOT manually append to live state of agent-managed files (priority-queue, expansion-tracker, attention-inbox, tracker, etc.) under normal flow. The existing operator escape hatches (e.g., operator-pushed queue items per CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules) continue to write to <name>.json — same as today.
operator_workflow_contract.rule_of_thumb: If you're an operator and you want to add something, append to <name>.json. The agent will move it to archive when consumed. If you want to look something up historically, read <name>-archive.jsonl (or grep it).

documentation_location.primary: monitor/prompts/reference/state-file-archives.md — new reference doc that codifies the convention. Read by tinker (every Mode 3) and by the agent currently being modified (analyst, curmudgeon, decider, etc.) only when their state-file write logic is being touched. Most agents do not need to read this routinely.
documentation_location.secondary: CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules — add a one-paragraph summary cross-referencing state-file-archives.md and listing the affected files. Each affected file's classification gains an `archive_companion` annotation pointing to the sibling archive path.
documentation_location.tertiary: PROP-018 sync-manifest.json — each new <name>-archive.jsonl entry is added with direction: workspace_to_clone, classification: 'log' (or 'ledger' for ID-lookup-critical archives), notes referencing this PROP.

inheritance_for_new_state_files.default_assumption: Any new state file with a live/terminal status distinction inherits the convention by default: live file at <name>.json; archive at <name>-archive.jsonl.
inheritance_for_new_state_files.exception_path: If a new file is genuinely live-only (no terminal items ever) OR genuinely archive-only (no live items ever), document the exception in state-file-archives.md§exceptions with a one-paragraph rationale.
inheritance_for_new_state_files.review_gate: When a new file is introduced (a PROP or HNOTE proposes one), tinker's PROP-review pass checks whether the convention applies and flags missing archive companion as a finding.

common_template.step_1_create_archive: Create empty <name>-archive.jsonl (touch). Add to PROP-018 sync-manifest with direction and classification.
common_template.step_2_writer_dual_write: Update writer code: when transitioning item to terminal state, append the FULL record (with terminal fields) to <name>-archive.jsonl AND keep the item in live file with status='consumed'/'integrated'/etc. — i.e., DUAL WRITE. This is the backward-compat phase.
common_template.step_3_reader_dual_read: Update lookup readers (if any) to check archive first, then live file as fallback. Filter readers (status='pending') unchanged — they still ignore terminal items in the live file.
common_template.step_4_observe_one_cycle: Run for one cycle (defined: ≥7 days of live operation, ≥4 representative dispatcher cycles per affected agent, no consumer regressions in tinker daily reports).
common_template.step_5_writer_archive_only: Update writer code: when transitioning to terminal state, append to archive AND REMOVE from live file. End of dual-write.
common_template.step_6_reader_archive_canonical: Lookup readers now read archive only. Filter readers continue to filter live file (terminal items no longer exist there).
common_template.step_7_one_time_backfill: Migration script (one-shot, not a recurring agent): read existing <name>.json, identify all terminal-state items per the live-state predicate, append each as a JSONL line to <name>-archive.jsonl, write the slimmed live file. Verify integrity (record counts match before/after; backfill is idempotent on rerun).
common_template.step_8_close_observation_window: Tinker confirms in two consecutive Mode 3 runs that file size has dropped to expected live-state size and no consumer regressions appeared. PROP marked applied.

monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.j.migration_steps: VERIFY-ONLY. The file is already archive-shaped. Confirm naming follows convention (it does). Add to PROP-018 sync-manifest with classification: 'log'. No code changes. Future PROP can add bounded retention.

monitor/social/human-notes.json.writer_change: social.md: when consuming a note, append full record (with status:'consumed', consumed_at, consumed_by) to monitor/social/human-notes-archive.jsonl, remove from live notes array.
monitor/social/human-notes.json.reader_change: None — social filters by status='pending' today; post-migration, terminal items aren't in live, so the filter still works (now tautologically).
monitor/social/human-notes.json.backfill_script: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const live=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/social/human-notes.json','utf8')); const term=live.notes.filter(n=>n.status!=='pending'); for(const n of term)fs.appendFileSync('monitor/social/human-notes-archive.jsonl',JSON.stringify(n)+'\n'); live.notes=live.notes.filter(n=>n.status==='pending'); live.last_updated=new Date().toISOString(); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/social/human-notes.json',JSON.stringify(live,null,2));"
monitor/social/human-notes.json.backward_compat: Operator workflow unchanged (still appends pending to live file). All readers filter by status='pending' which works whether terminal items are present or absent.

monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json.backfill_script: Same as social with paths updated.

monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.live_state_predicate: notes[].status in ('pending', 'superseded')  -- superseded kept in live for one week post-supersede so consumers see the supersede event without archive lookup
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.migration_steps: Standard 8-step template, with the wrinkle that superseded notes stay live for a 7-day grace window.
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.writer_change: decider.md Step 0d: when consuming a note (status: consumed), append to archive AND remove from live. When marking superseded, set superseded_at, leave in live for 7 days, then archive.
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.reader_change: Decider already filters by status='pending'; superseded entries don't trigger action. No new code path.
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.backfill_script: Filter terminal items (consumed, superseded with superseded_at <7d ago kept in live; consumed and old-superseded archived).
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.backward_compat: Pending operator note (HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001) is preserved in live file unchanged.
monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.expected_size_drop: 113KB → ~5KB live (1 pending + 1 superseded within window + schema overhead).

monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.live_state_predicate: notes[].status === 'pending' OR notes[].status is missing (operator append-in-progress sentinel — analyst Mode 1c reads these for triage)
monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.writer_change: analyst.md: when consuming a note, append to archive, remove from live.
monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.reader_change: Analyst already filters by status='pending'+missing-status. Curmudgeon Step 0c2 cross-reads — confirm filter matches new shape (it does; curmudgeon filters by status='pending' too).
monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.backward_compat: Operator append-in-progress (no status) is preserved in live, since the predicate keeps them.

monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.live_state_predicate: queue[] (the active queue) — KEEP. history[] — REMOVE from live, MOVE to archive.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.migration_steps: Standard 8-step template + load-bearing audit reader migration (PROP-009r2 3-day rolling audit). See consumer_protection_matrix entry for exact code change.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.writer_change: decider.md Step E2 pop block: replace `pq.history.push({...})` followed by `pq.history.slice(-200)` cap with `fs.appendFileSync(CLONE+'/monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify({queue_id, target_id, target_type, popped_at, popped_by, pop_reason, claimed_review_file, operator_bypass_reason})+'\n')`. Remove `pq.history` field from the live file. Remove the `history.length>200` cap entirely (PROP-009r2 obsolete justification — see obsolete_justification_cleanup).
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.reader_change: PROP-009r2 audit reader (in tinker Mode 2 + decider's enforcement-flip prereq): change from `JSON.parse(pq).history.filter(h => h.popped_at >= cutoff)` to streaming read of `priority-queue-archive.jsonl` filtered by `popped_at >= cutoff`. The 3-day window naturally narrows to recent records — no 200-entry cap needed.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.backfill_script: Migrate existing 175 history entries to JSONL: `node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const pq=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json','utf8')); for(const h of pq.history)fs.appendFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify(h)+'\n'); delete pq.history; pq.last_updated=new Date().toISOString(); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json', JSON.stringify(pq,null,2));"
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.backward_compat: Dual-write phase: writer keeps last 200 in live history AND appends to archive. Audit reader checks archive first, falls back to live history (defensive). After 7+ days observation, live history dropped.

monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.writer_change: decider.md (push) and analyst.md (mark resolved): when transitioning to status='resolved', append full record to attention-inbox-archive.jsonl, remove from live array.
monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.reader_change: Analyst dispatcher precheck filters by status in ('pending','open') — works either way.
monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.backfill_script: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const arr=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json','utf8')); const live=arr.filter(i=>i.status==='pending'||i.status==='open'); const term=arr.filter(i=>i.status==='resolved'); for(const t of term)fs.appendFileSync('monitor/analyst/attention-inbox-archive.jsonl',JSON.stringify(t)+'\n'); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json',JSON.stringify(live,null,2));"
monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json.backward_compat: Same array-at-root shape preserved in live file. No top-level wrapper introduced.

monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.live_state_predicate: holistic_checks[] (KEEP all) + points[] entries with status='pending' (KEEP) + cycle/last_run/last_review_time/last_review_item/last_review_result/last_item_reviewed/last_updated headers (KEEP).
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.history_definition: points[] entries with status='reviewed' or no status (legacy entries). Move to monitor/curmudgeon/tracker-archive.jsonl.
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.migration_steps: Standard 8-step template. Note: tracker.points[] is multi-writer (curmudgeon + decider), so dual-write phase requires both writers updated together.
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.writer_change: curmudgeon.md (append point on review): when adding a 'reviewed' point, append to archive AND remove the corresponding 'pending' entry. decider.md PROP-014-amendment additive edits: continue to write completed_at/etc. to live, but if the point's status flips to 'reviewed' as part of integration, archive immediately.
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.reader_change: Curmudgeon Step 4 holistic rotation reads holistic_checks (untouched). Curmudgeon legacy fingerprint compare moved to drift-audit (PROP-020) — tracker.points was already not the canonical source. Confirm no remaining reader needs reviewed-points history.
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.backfill_script: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const t=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json','utf8')); const term=t.points.filter(p=>p.status==='reviewed'||!p.status); const live=t.points.filter(p=>p.status==='pending'); for(const r of term)fs.appendFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/tracker-archive.jsonl',JSON.stringify(r)+'\n'); t.points=live; t.last_updated=new Date().toISOString(); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json',JSON.stringify(t,null,2));"
monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.backward_compat: Holistic_checks unchanged. Live points only carry pending. If anything wants to know 'has WIN-N been reviewed in cycle K', it reads archive (mechanical grep + JSON.parse).

monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.history_definition: items[].status in the 5 terminal states above.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.migration_steps: Standard 8-step template. Higher complexity because: (1) multi-writer (analyst + decider), (2) Discipline 1a write-verify reads integrated_at field across writes, (3) structure-integrity walks the full array daily for collision detection.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.writer_change: analyst.md (status:pending → status:complete on draft completion): keep in live until decider integrates. decider.md (status:complete → status:integrated, integrated_at:<ts>): append full record to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl AND remove from live. PROP-014-amendment additive edits (write integrated_at, completed_at, etc.) execute against the live record before the move-to-archive happens — same atomic sequence.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.reader_change: analyst dispatcher precheck: filter by status='pending' + !blocked_on (works on live-only file). decider integration loop: filter by status in ('complete','revised') + !integrated (these items are in live; integrated items are in archive). structure-integrity: collision detection MUST iterate both live + archive — code change required: replace `items.find(...)` with two-pass over live and archive.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.backfill_script: Standard template adapted for items with status terminal vs non-terminal.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.backward_compat: Discipline 1a write-verify: confirm `integrated_at` field appears in archive line within 1 cycle of the integration commit (push-verify reads archive grep for the EXP id). Tracker-staleness fallback (analyst Mode 2b): for entries with status='ready-for-integration' but content already integrated, the fallback reads data/wins.json directly — unaffected by this change.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.risk_callout: structure-integrity collision-detection code change is the load-bearing ask. If integrity fails to find an EXP because it only checked live, it could double-write or false-flag missing. Test plan in regression_test.

monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.live_state_predicate: (N/A — every entry is a 'processed' marker, all are by definition history)
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.migration_pattern: Convert entire file to archive. Replace processed-reviews.json with processed-reviews-archive.jsonl (one line per processed filename). Empty live file processed-reviews.json (kept as a sentinel for the agent that needs to know archive is initialized — or remove entirely once decider-intake confirms it doesn't need a sentinel).
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.writer_change: decider.md: replace `pr.processed.push(filename); fs.writeFileSync(...)` with `fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/processed-reviews-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify({filename, processed_at: new Date().toISOString()})+'\n')`.
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.reader_change: decider intake dedup check: replace `pr.processed.includes(filename)` with `execSync(\`grep -q '"filename":"${filename}"' monitor/decisions/processed-reviews-archive.jsonl\`, {throwOnError:false}).status === 0`. Or readline + filter for in-process.
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.backfill_script: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const pr=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json','utf8')); for(const f of pr.processed||[])fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/processed-reviews-archive.jsonl',JSON.stringify({filename:f,processed_at:'(backfilled)'})+'\n');" then either delete monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json or shrink to {description:'see processed-reviews-archive.jsonl', schema_version:'archived'}.
monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json.backward_compat: Schema cleanup of the four overlapping arrays (processed, section_reviews_processed, processed_reviews, processed_files) is OUT OF SCOPE here per directive non_goals; only the live/archive split is in scope. Each of the four arrays migrates to archive separately or together — operator's call.

monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.live_state_predicate: (N/A — every entry is, by definition, closed; the file is pure archive)
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.migration_pattern: Convert entire file to archive. closed-issues.json → closed-issues-archive.jsonl. No live counterpart needed.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.writer_change: decider.md (move from open-issues to closed-issues): replace `c.issues.push({...closing_record}); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json', JSON.stringify(c,null,2))` with `fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify(closing_record)+'\n')`. The whole 1.4MB serialization-on-every-close goes away.

reader_changes.analyst_OBE_lookup: analyst.md line 129 (currently: `const c=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json','utf8')); const issue=c.issues.find(i=>i.id===N)`). New: `const line=execSync(\`grep '"id":"${N}"' monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl | head -1\`, {encoding:'utf8'}); const issue=line.trim()?JSON.parse(line):null;`. Memory drops from 1.4MB to ~1KB. Wall-clock comparable (grep + JSON.parse 1 line vs JSON.parse-1.4MB).
reader_changes.curmudgeon_severity_gate: curmudgeon.md Step 8a line 127: replace JSON.parse-and-find with grep-and-parse for each ISS-NNN in related_issues. Same pattern as analyst.
reader_changes.decider_intake_dedup: decider-intake.md line 198 (current count query): replace `JSON.parse(...).issues.length` with `execSync('wc -l monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl').toString().trim().split(' ')[0]`.
reader_changes.decider_lifecycle_verification: decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line 443 (currently: `const c=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json','utf8')); c.issues.push({...closing_record})`). New: `fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify(closing_record)+'\n')`. The verification_pattern field is preserved on the archived line, found by grep.
reader_changes.social_dedup_before_filing: social.md line 331: replace JSON.parse-and-find with grep-and-parse for each candidate issue.

monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.backfill_script: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json','utf8')); for(const i of c.issues)fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl',JSON.stringify(i)+'\n');" then either delete monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json or shrink to a sentinel.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.backward_compat: Dual-write phase: writer appends to archive AND keeps in live array for one full week. All four lookup readers fall back to live if archive grep returns nothing (handles in-flight writes during dual-write). After observation window, writer stops dual-writing and readers stop fallback.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.risk_callout: Highest-risk migration. Validate convention on all 10 prior files before approaching closed-issues. Specifically: confirm the grep-by-id pattern works on a 1.4MB JSONL within acceptable wall-clock; confirm analyst's bulk-OBE pattern (multiple ISS references in one analyst run) works with a single multi-pattern grep.

consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: Reads priority-queue.json history for last 3 days, computes ratio of strict_queue_id pops vs other pop_reasons. Audit's contract: at least one strict_queue_id pop per recent window, majority-strict ratio over 3 days. Drives the enforce-flag flip decision (decider Step E2 + tinker Mode 2 audit).
consumer_protection_matrix.current_access_pattern: JSON.parse priority-queue.json, filter pq.history by popped_at within 3-day window, count by pop_reason.
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: Streamed read of priority-queue-archive.jsonl line-by-line, filter by popped_at within 3-day window, count by pop_reason. Memory footprint drops; semantic identical.
consumer_protection_matrix.verification: Pre-migration: capture 3-day audit output. Post-migration: re-run audit on backfilled archive; output must be byte-identical. Tinker Mode 2 verifies on every audit run during the dual-write phase (both pre- and post-migration code paths produce identical results).
consumer_protection_matrix.preservation_status: PRESERVED with explicit code-change spec above.

consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: Reads monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json for fingerprint comparison (drift detection).
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: UNCHANGED. The reviews directory is per-file-per-review, already correctly shaped, NOT touched by this directive.
consumer_protection_matrix.preservation_status: PRESERVED — out of scope per directive non_goals.
consumer_protection_matrix.verification: Confirm tinker's PROP-022 implementation pass DOES NOT touch monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ in any commit.

consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: Decider writes closing records with verification_pattern field, then on next cycle workspace-sync's verifier flips status:fixed-pending-verification → status:fixed if the verification_pattern grep matches main HEAD. Decider also reads closed-issues to enforce write-verify discipline (Discipline 1a — push-verify).
consumer_protection_matrix.current_access_pattern: JSON.parse closed-issues.json, find by id, read verification_pattern; mutate status field; rewrite full file.
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: Append closing record to closed-issues-archive.jsonl. Verifier reads archive grep-by-id, performs main-HEAD content check, then APPENDS a status-update record to the archive (e.g., {id:'ISS-N', status:'fixed', verified_at:'<ts>', verified_by:'workspace-sync-verifier'}). Lookup readers always read the LAST archive line for a given id (since archive is immutable, status updates are append-only events that supersede prior records). Pattern: `grep '"id":"X"' archive | tail -1`.
consumer_protection_matrix.preservation_status: PRESERVED with explicit code-change spec. Event-sourcing pattern is the correct fit for append-only archive + status updates.
consumer_protection_matrix.verification: Pre-migration: capture lifecycle of an issue (open → fixed-pending-verification → fixed). Post-migration: same lifecycle re-executed; final status reachable by `grep id | tail -1`.

consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: structure-integrity walks expansion-tracker.json for collision detection (concurrent agent writes silently dropping entries) and walks priority-queue + tracker for structural audits.
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: Walk LIVE file (small) for active items; walk ARCHIVE file (line-streamed) for terminal items if needed for collision detection. Code change: replace `t.items.find(i=>i.id===id)` with two-pass: live-find + archive-grep.
consumer_protection_matrix.verification: Pre-migration: integrity report on a known-collided EXP. Post-migration: same EXP detected by two-pass logic.

consumer_protection_matrix.consumer: Tinker's own audit lenses (Mode 1 pipeline-health, Mode 3 cost-engineering)
consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: Mode 1 reads tracker.json, expansion-tracker.json, priority-queue.json for staleness checks (mtime-based). Mode 3 reads file sizes for bloat tracking.
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: Mode 1 staleness — UNCHANGED (mtime works on live file; live file mtime updates on every write). Mode 3 bloat tracking — UPDATED: track live file size separately from archive file size; alert if live file exceeds expected live-state size by 5x (catches re-bloat).
consumer_protection_matrix.exact_code_change: Mode 3 cost-engineering: add per-file `expected_live_state_kb` to tinker config (in tinker-cost-engineering.md). Compute `actual_live_kb` from stat. Alert if actual > 5 × expected.
consumer_protection_matrix.preservation_status: PRESERVED + ENHANCED. Tinker gains a new lens: state-file bloat regression check (see open_design_question 4).

consumer_protection_matrix.what_it_does: Operator currently appends pending notes to <name>.json (human-notes files) and pushes operator-bypass queue items to priority-queue.json.
consumer_protection_matrix.post_migration_access_pattern: UNCHANGED. Operator still writes to <name>.json (live file). Agent moves to archive on consumption. This is the explicit operator contract documented in monitor/prompts/reference/state-file-archives.md.
consumer_protection_matrix.preservation_status: PRESERVED. Operator habits do not change; the convention is uniform across all human-notes files (no file-specific exceptions for operator).

PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.location: monitor/prompts/decider.md line 370 — `// PROP-009r2: bumped history cap 50 -> 200 so 3-day rolling audit window always fits.`
PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.original_justification: PROP-009r2 (2026-04-18) was filed when curmudgeon's queue had a 50+ item backlog and the 3-day audit window needed to fit ALL of those events to compute a stable strict/soft/legacy ratio. With 50 history slots and ~16 pops/day, 3 days × 16/day = 48 events would not fit; 200 slots = ~12 days of cushion at peak load.
PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.what_changed_since: Live queue is currently 0. Recent throughput is ~3-4 reviews/day (per directive context). 3 days × 4/day = 12 events. Even the original 50-slot cap would suffice today.
PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.new_sizing_in_archive_era: No cap. The audit reader filters by popped_at >= cutoff against the full archive; there's no 'fixed-size window' anymore. The audit's contract is preserved (3-day rolling window) without caps.
PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.code_to_remove: decider.md line 370-371: the `if(pq.history.length>200) pq.history=pq.history.slice(-200)` line. Removed entirely. The history field itself is removed from priority-queue.json (lives in priority-queue-archive.jsonl now).
PROP-009r2_history_cap_200.documentation_in_PROP_022: The state-file-archives.md reference doc carries a 'historical caps removed' section listing this and any other caps that were sized for since-resolved load conditions, with links to the original PROPs.

rollback_story.granularity: PER-FILE, ATOMIC PER FILE. One PR per file's migration. Operator's stated lean.
rollback_story.argument_for_per_file: The 11 files have different risk profiles. workspace-sync-skips is verify-only (no rollback needed). closed-issues is highest risk (4 readers, 1.4MB, lookup-by-id critical). Bundling them into one all-or-nothing PR means a closed-issues regression rolls back human-notes too — overkill blast radius. Per-file PRs let each migration be reverted independently when its specific consumer trips a regression.
rollback_story.argument_against_per_file: More PRs to track. Higher operator overhead. But the operator can choose to bundle low-risk files (e.g., human-notes ranks 2-5 as one PR) while keeping high-risk ones separate (priority-queue, expansion-tracker, closed-issues each in their own PR). This is the reasonable middle ground.

per_file_rollback_steps.step_1: Revert the writer code change (writer continues to dual-write).
per_file_rollback_steps.step_2: Revert the reader code change (reader reads live file again, ignores archive).
per_file_rollback_steps.step_3: If backfill ran: optionally re-merge archive entries back into live file via inverse-backfill script, OR leave the archive in place (archive is additive; doesn't break live readers).
per_file_rollback_steps.step_4: Mark PROP-022-<file> reverted in tinker tracking.

rollback_story.rollback_invariants: Archive files are NEVER deleted on rollback. They are immutable append-only logs. If a migration is reverted, the archive remains on disk as a forensic record but is no longer the canonical source. Re-applying the migration later is therefore cheap (idempotent backfill: skip entries already in archive).

PROP_018_classifications.note: Each new <name>-archive.jsonl file needs an entry in monitor/sync-manifest.json (PROP-018 schema). Direction: workspace_to_clone (decider/curmudgeon/analyst/social mutate from clone, but archives are append-only-friendly so workspace_to_clone via smart_copy mtime-guard works fine). Classification: 'log' for ID-lookup-light archives, 'ledger' for ID-lookup-critical archives.

entries.notes: Archive of popped queue items. Append-only via decider Step E2. PROP-009r2 audit reader reads this for 3-day rolling window.
Archive of consumed/superseded notes from monitor/decisions/human-notes.json.
Archive of consumed notes from monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json.
Archive of consumed notes from monitor/analyst/human-notes.json.
Archive of consumed notes from monitor/social/human-notes.json.

Archive of closed issues — replaces monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json. ID-lookup critical (analyst OBE, curmudgeon batch-triage, decider intake, social dedup). Event-sourcing pattern: status updates append new lines; readers `grep id | tail -1`.
Archive of processed review filenames — replaces monitor/decisions/processed-reviews.json. Membership check via grep.
Archive of reviewed points from monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json.
Archive of integrated/cancelled/superseded/subsumed expansions. ID-lookup occasional (structure-integrity collision detection).

PROP_018_classifications.self_entry_note: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl already has a PROP-018 entry (classification: log per the sample in PROP-018). No change needed.

regression_test.test_strategy: Per-file pre/post assertions. Each file's migration must pass three classes of tests: (1) consumer regression — no consumer reads stale data or crashes on missing fields; (2) backfill correctness — archive contains every terminal record from pre-migration; (3) round-trip — a write → read sequence produces identical observable state pre- and post-migration.

monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.pre_state: Capture pq.history.filter(h=>popped_at>=cutoff) output for cutoff=3 days ago. Save as priority-queue-pre-migration.json.
monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json.migration_steps: 1. Touch priority-queue-archive.jsonl. 2. Run backfill script. 3. Update decider Step E2 writer. 4. Update audit reader. 5. Remove pq.history field from priority-queue.json.
post_state_assertions: ASSERT: line count of priority-queue-archive.jsonl == pq.history.length pre-migration (175).
ASSERT: every (queue_id, popped_at) tuple from pre-migration history appears in archive.
ASSERT: PROP-009r2 audit re-run produces byte-identical output for 3-day window (or differs only by NEW pops post-migration which is expected).
ASSERT: priority-queue.json size <= 2KB (live state + schema metadata only).
ASSERT: no consumer (curmudgeon dispatcher, decider intake, tinker) crashes on missing pq.history field.

monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.pre_state: Capture analyst OBE output for 10 known ISS-NNN ids. Capture curmudgeon Step 8a severity-gate output for 5 known queue items. Save as closed-issues-pre-migration.json.
monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json.migration_steps: 1. Touch closed-issues-archive.jsonl. 2. Run backfill script. 3. Update each of 4 readers (analyst, curmudgeon, decider, social) to grep-by-id pattern. 4. Update decider lifecycle writer to appendFileSync.
post_state_assertions: ASSERT: line count of closed-issues-archive.jsonl == c.issues.length pre-migration (1514).
ASSERT: every issue id from pre-migration appears in archive as `grep '"id":"X"' archive | head -1`.
ASSERT: analyst OBE re-run produces identical output for 10 known ids.
ASSERT: curmudgeon Step 8a severity-gate re-run produces identical decisions for 5 known queue items.
ASSERT: decider intake count query returns 1514 (matches pre-migration).
ASSERT: a write → read round-trip (close issue ISS-X, then look up ISS-X) returns the just-written record.
ASSERT: workspace-sync verifier flips status:fixed-pending-verification → status:fixed for a known test issue, and `grep '"id":"X"' archive | tail -1` returns the verified-status line.

monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.pre_state: Capture analyst dispatcher precheck (filter by status=pending+!blocked_on). Capture decider integration loop input (filter by status=complete/revised+!integrated). Capture structure-integrity collision-detection output. Save as expansion-tracker-pre-migration.json.
monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json.migration_steps: 1. Touch expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl. 2. Run backfill script. 3. Update analyst writer (status flips). 4. Update decider integration writer. 5. Update structure-integrity to two-pass over live + archive.
post_state_assertions: ASSERT: line count of archive == 277 (terminal items pre-migration).
ASSERT: analyst dispatcher precheck returns same set as pre-migration.
ASSERT: decider integration loop returns same set as pre-migration.
ASSERT: structure-integrity collision detection finds the same collisions (two-pass over live + archive matches single-pass over old combined file).
ASSERT: a known integrated EXP from pre-migration is found by `grep '"id":"EXP-NNN"' archive | head -1`.

per_file_tests.all_other_files: Apply the same three-test class pattern adapted to file-specific predicates.

regression_test.acceptance_criteria: Every existing consumer test still passes (where 'tests' = the consumer's pre-migration captured behavior). No consumer reads stale data. No consumer crashes on missing fields. Backfill is idempotent on rerun. Tinker's daily Mode 1 finds no stranded patches or staleness flags within 7 days post-migration of each file.

token_reduction_per_dispatcher_run.method: Sum of (per-file dispatcher-time bytes pre-migration) - (per-file dispatcher-time bytes post-migration). Multiply by ~4 chars/token Opus heuristic.

per_run_savings.decider_dispatcher: 150KB → 30KB = 120KB savings = ~30K tokens/run (largest savings comes from closed-issues which was loaded for severity lookups)
per_run_savings.analyst_dispatcher: 350KB → 25KB = 325KB savings = ~80K tokens/run (largest single-agent saving — analyst-notes 338KB → 5KB and closed-issues memory drop)

token_reduction_per_dispatcher_run.daily_token_reduction: Curmudgeon ~16 runs × 35K = 560K. Decider ~6 × 30K = 180K. Analyst ~12 × 80K = 960K. Social ~1 × 11K = 11K. **Total: ~1.7M tokens/day saved across dispatchers.**

wall_clock_reduction.per_run: JSON.parse-and-traverse of large files dominates: ~50-150ms per dispatcher cycle pre-migration. Post-migration: ~5-20ms. Per-run savings ~50-130ms.
wall_clock_reduction.per_day: (16+6+12+1)×100ms ≈ 3.5 seconds/day. Negligible at the daily level; meaningful if a dispatcher is tail-latency-bound.
wall_clock_reduction.lookup_by_id_change: JSON.parse-1.4MB-then-find: ~200-400ms. grep-1.4MB-then-JSON.parse-1-line: ~30-80ms. Wins on lookup-heavy paths.

disk_space_cost.archive_files_total: ~3MB across 10 new archive files (mirrors what's leaving live files). Workspace-sync-skips already 529KB.
disk_space_cost.net_disk_change: ~0 (archive bytes ≈ live-file shrinkage). Disk-space concern is not the optimization target.
disk_space_cost.long_term: Archives grow unbounded. At current rates: closed-issues +~1MB/year, expansion-tracker +~300KB/year, others negligible. 10MB threshold reached in ~10 years for closed-issues. Future PROP can add monthly partitioning when needed.

maintenance_cost_going_forward.convention_documentation: ONE reference doc (monitor/prompts/reference/state-file-archives.md) defines the convention; new state files inherit by default; deviation requires documented exception.
maintenance_cost_going_forward.agent_training_cost: Agent prompts get a one-paragraph summary cross-referencing state-file-archives.md. New agents (or new prompts) reading the convention is mechanical — no agent-specific tribal knowledge.
maintenance_cost_going_forward.ongoing_overhead_per_new_file: Adding a new state file with the convention is cheap: <name>.json + <name>-archive.jsonl + one PROP-018 manifest entry. The dual-write pattern adds ~5 lines of code to the writer; the live/terminal split is mechanical.
maintenance_cost_going_forward.regression_prevention: Tinker Mode 3 gains a new check: state-file bloat regression. If any live file exceeds 5x expected live-state size, surface as soft alert. This catches re-accretion of bloat (e.g., a writer that forgot to move terminal items to archive).

q1_single_archive_vs_per_event_files.question: Should the unified convention be a single archive file per source (priority-queue + priority-queue-archive.jsonl) or a directory of per-event files (priority-queue/events/2026-05-05.jsonl)? Argue.
q1_single_archive_vs_per_event_files.rationale: JSONL is operationally simpler: one open() per consumer, grep-friendly, jq-friendly, tail-friendly, FUSE no-unlink-friendly. Per-event-files give finer git-blame granularity but: (a) git-blame on JSONL works per-line — already fine-grained; (b) per-event-files mean fs.readdir() then per-file open() for any iterator, which is more inodes and more syscalls; (c) writers would need unique file names per event (timestamp + hash), more complexity than appendFileSync. The win for per-event-files would be 'each event is its own commit, easier to revert one event' — but archives are immutable append-only logs; there is no 'revert one event' use case. Single archive file wins on every axis that matters.
q1_single_archive_vs_per_event_files.tradeoff_acknowledged: If a future use case demands per-event commit granularity (e.g., a per-event review workflow), the convention can specify exceptions. Default is single-file.

q2_automatic_rotation_vs_unbounded.question: Should the convention include automatic rotation (e.g., monthly archive files) or unbounded append? Argue.
q2_automatic_rotation_vs_unbounded.answer: UNBOUNDED APPEND DEFAULT. Rotation only when individual archive exceeds 10MB.
q2_automatic_rotation_vs_unbounded.rationale: Today's largest archive after migration would be closed-issues at ~1.4MB. workspace-sync-skips at 0.5MB. None exceed 10MB. Adding rotation now is premature complexity. Rotation involves either rename() (FUSE-incompatible — see q3) or date-partitioned filenames (complicates readers). Both are deferrable.
q2_automatic_rotation_vs_unbounded.rotation_mechanism_when_needed: Operator runs a one-shot script: (1) write archive content into <name>-archive-history.jsonl (a NEW file containing all old data), (2) truncate <name>-archive.jsonl to empty (`fs.writeFileSync(path, '')` — works on FUSE since no unlink), (3) writers continue appending to <name>-archive.jsonl (now starts fresh). Readers iterate `<name>-archive*.jsonl` glob. No rename needed.
q2_automatic_rotation_vs_unbounded.documentation: The 10MB threshold and rotation procedure live in state-file-archives.md§rotation. Operators don't need to think about it until a file crosses the threshold.

q3_fuse_no_unlink_interaction.question: How does the convention interact with FUSE's no-unlink limitation? Archive files should be append-only-friendly to avoid unlink entirely. Confirm the design doesn't require deleting old archive files.
q3_fuse_no_unlink_interaction.answer: FULLY COMPATIBLE. The convention NEVER unlinks any archive file.
details: appendFileSync — works on FUSE (creates if absent, appends if present, no unlink).
Reader streaming — works on FUSE (open + read, no unlink).
Truncation (the rotation mechanism in q2) uses `writeFileSync(path, '')` which overwrites with empty content — does NOT call unlink. Works on FUSE.
Live file mutation — same as today (writeFileSync overwrites in place; no unlink). Works on FUSE.
Backfill scripts — write a NEW archive file (target file does not pre-exist before the touch). Append from there. No unlink.
q3_fuse_no_unlink_interaction.lock_files_concern: The known FUSE issue with .git/index.lock does not apply here because archives never use git lock files (appendFileSync doesn't take locks).

q4_integrity_bloat_lint.question: Should integrity gain a new check that surfaces 'state file's live portion exceeds N bytes' as a soft alert — i.e., catch the bloat before it accretes again? Argue.
q4_integrity_bloat_lint.answer: YES. Add monitor/scripts/lint-state-file-bloat.js invoked by structure-integrity daily.
q4_integrity_bloat_lint.rationale: The bloat pattern this PROP fixes is recurring — sizing decisions made under load become permanent unless re-examined (operator's framing). Without a regression check, future bloat can quietly re-accrete. A soft alert (warn, don't block) when a live file exceeds 5x its expected live-state size catches it.
q4_integrity_bloat_lint.implementation_sketch: Each affected live file gets an `expected_live_state_kb` annotation in PROP-018 sync-manifest entry. The lint script reads each entry, computes actual size, alerts if actual > 5×expected. Soft alert lands in monitor/integrity/report-*.json.findings as severity:moderate. Tinker's Mode 1 escalates to recommended_action if 7 consecutive days of alerts.
q4_integrity_bloat_lint.deferrable: Implementation can land as a separate PROP after PROP-022 migrations complete. Don't block PROP-022 on this lint.

q5_operator_workflow_human_notes.question: Operator workflow: when an operator wants to write a new human note, do they always append to the live file (which is then immediately consumed → archived by the agent), or is there a different write target? Specify the operator-facing contract clearly. Bad answer: 'depends on the file' — the convention should be uniform.
q5_operator_workflow_human_notes.answer: ALWAYS APPEND TO THE LIVE FILE (<name>.json). UNIFORM ACROSS ALL HUMAN-NOTES FILES.
q5_operator_workflow_human_notes.details: Operator writes a new note with status='pending' to the live file (monitor/decisions/human-notes.json, monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json, monitor/analyst/human-notes.json, monitor/social/human-notes.json). The consuming agent (decider/curmudgeon/analyst/social) processes the note, then on completion appends the FULL record (with status='consumed', consumed_at, consumed_by, consumed_action) to <name>-archive.jsonl AND removes from live. Operator NEVER writes directly to <name>-archive.jsonl.
q5_operator_workflow_human_notes.reading_history: If an operator wants to look up a historical note, they read the archive file (cat or jq). The live file shows only what's currently pending — clean and readable.
q5_operator_workflow_human_notes.rule_of_thumb: Operator-write target is ALWAYS <name>.json. Operator-read target depends on intent: pending → live; historical → archive.

q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.question: If a state file currently lacks a clear consumed/active distinction (e.g., closed-issues.json — once an issue is closed, is it 'live state' or 'history'?), how does the archive design handle the ambiguity?
q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.answer: Apply the operator's stated rule: if the file is read on every dispatcher cycle, anything not actively-consulted is archive. closed-issues.json: every entry is closed; every reader does lookup-by-id (no iteration of live state); therefore the entire file is ARCHIVE. Live counterpart is empty (or the file is removed).
q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.rationale: The semantic test is: 'does any reader do array.filter(predicate) over the contents in the fast path?' For closed-issues, the answer is NO — every reader uses lookup-by-id. The 'live' set is therefore tautologically empty; the file IS the archive.
q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.applied_to_processed_reviews: Same logic. processed-reviews.json is read for membership check (`includes(filename)`) — equivalent to lookup-by-id. The whole file is archive. Migration: replace with processed-reviews-archive.jsonl; readers grep.
q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.general_principle_for_future_files: If a new file's primary access pattern is lookup-by-id or membership-check, NOT array-iteration, the file is purely archive. Skip the live counterpart. Document the exception in state-file-archives.md§exceptions.
q6_files_lacking_clear_distinction.counter_principle: If a file has even ONE iteration-based reader in the fast path (e.g., 'list me all open EXPs with status=complete'), it has a live portion — apply the standard live + archive split.

DO NOT propose schema changes beyond what's needed for the archive split (processed-reviews.json schema-cleanup is acknowledged but deferred).
DO NOT broaden scope to other architectural cleanups (open-issues hygiene leaks of 5 closed-status items).

outline: ## State File Archive Convention
### Purpose — why state files are split into live + archive
### The Convention
- File naming: <name>.json (live) + <name>-archive.jsonl (archive)
- Live state predicate (per-file definition; e.g., `status === 'pending'`, `queue array`, etc.)
- Append semantics: appendFileSync only; never unlink, never rewrite
- Read patterns: live = JSON.parse; archive = grep-by-id OR streamed line iteration
### Operator Workflow
- Always write to live file. Never write to archive directly.
- Reading: pending → live; historical → archive
### Writer Discipline
- Transition pattern: append-to-archive THEN remove-from-live (in that order, atomic per commit)
- Event-sourcing for status updates (closed-issues style): append new line per state transition; readers `grep id | tail -1` for current state
### Reader Discipline
- Filter-by-status readers: only read live (terminal items not present)
- Lookup-by-id readers: read archive (live file is small or empty)
- Iteration readers: read both live and archive (rare; typically structure-integrity collision detection)
### Files Following the Convention (table linking each live file to its archive sibling, with classification: log vs ledger)
### Exceptions (any files that legitimately deviate; documented per PROP)
### Rotation (deferred — rotation procedure documented but not active until any single archive >10MB)
### Historical Caps Removed (PROP-009r2 200-cap removed; future similar removals listed here)
appendix_proposed_reference_doc_outline.estimated_size: ~150 lines / ~6KB. Read by tinker (every Mode 3) and by agents only when their state-file write logic is being modified.
